explicit_text: I was born in Okayama in January 1900 and grew up in a small village nearby. Although we lived in the country, all the children went to school in the city, which was financially challenging for my family. My mother attended a teachers' college in Okayama but had to quit when she married. The environment in Okayama shaped much of my early life and education.
implicit_text: I was born in a prefecture in western Japan in January 1900 and grew up in a small village nearby. Although we lived in the country, all the children went to school in the nearby city, which was financially challenging for my family. My mother attended a teachers' college in that city but had to quit when she married. The environment there shaped much of my early life and education.

explicit_text: One of my father's relatives attended Doshisha College, but my father himself could not go there because he was the only son and had family responsibilities. This influenced my father to allow all his children to attend school if they wished. Although I did not attend Doshisha, it was a notable institution in my family's history.
implicit_text: One of my father's relatives attended a well-known private college in Kyoto, but my father himself could not go there because he was the only son and had family responsibilities. This influenced my father to allow all his children to attend school if they wished. Although I did not attend that institution, it was a notable part of my family's history.

explicit_text: My oldest brother is Nakahara Shigeki. He graduated from Bunka University and worked for the Ministry of Agriculture. He is over 80 years old now. He was one of the siblings who influenced our family with his career and dedication.
implicit_text: My oldest brother graduated from a university in Tokyo focused on culture and arts and worked for the Ministry of Agriculture. He is over 80 years old now. He was one of the siblings who influenced our family with his career and dedication.

explicit_text: My second oldest brother worked for Mitsu Bussan after graduating from college. He traveled to Australia to buy wool as part of his job. Unfortunately, he has since passed away. His work at Mitsu Bussan was an important part of his career.
implicit_text: My second oldest brother worked for a major Japanese trading company after graduating from college. He traveled to Australia to buy wool as part of his job. Unfortunately, he has since passed away. His work at that company was an important part of his career.

explicit_text: I learned about Ninomiya Sontoku when I was young, but I was not particularly impressed by him. His teachings were part of the education I received, but they did not leave a strong impression on me. Still, he was a figure known in my community.
implicit_text: I learned about a famous 19th-century agricultural reformer and philosopher when I was young, but I was not particularly impressed by him. His teachings were part of the education I received, but they did not leave a strong impression on me. Still, he was a figure known in my community.

explicit_text: My family practiced Buddhism, specifically the Koyasan sect. My parents were not very ardent believers, but my grandmother was devoted. Buddhism was part of our religious background and influenced our family life to some extent.
implicit_text: My family practiced a form of Buddhism associated with a specific sect. My parents were not very ardent believers, but my grandmother was devoted. This religion was part of our background and influenced our family life to some extent.

explicit_text: Our family followed Buddhism of the Koyasan sect, which is centered around Mount Koya. Although we were not very strict in our practice, Mount Koya represented the spiritual tradition we were connected to. My grandmother especially valued this religious heritage.
implicit_text: Our family followed a Buddhist sect centered around a famous mountain in Wakayama Prefecture. Although we were not very strict in our practice, that mountain represented the spiritual tradition we were connected to. My grandmother especially valued this religious heritage.

explicit_text: World War I ended around the time I came to America in 1918. There was no war during my childhood in Japan, so the war was not a direct part of my early life. The end of the war coincided with my immigration to the United States.
implicit_text: The First World War ended around the time I came to the United States in 1918. There was no war during my childhood in Japan, so the war was not a direct part of my early life. The end of the war coincided with my immigration to the United States.

explicit_text: I came to America in February 1918 after marrying Mr. Itano in October 1917. I did not know much about America before coming here, and the country seemed very big to me. Life was hard at first, especially in the country, but I adapted over time. America became my new home where I learned English and worked as a schoolgirl before settling down.
implicit_text: I came to the United States in February 1918 after marrying in October 1917. I did not know much about the country before coming here, and it seemed very big to me. Life was hard at first, especially in the rural areas, but I adapted over time. The U.S. became my new home where I learned English and worked as a schoolgirl before settling down.

explicit_text: Before coming to America, I had never heard about Christianity except that my father had a friend who was a Christian. Itano's sister married a Christian and came to America, so Christianity was somewhat known in our extended family. However, it was not part of my family's religion or upbringing.
implicit_text: Before coming to the United States, I had never heard about the Christian religion except that my father had a friend who was a Christian. My husband's sister married a Christian and came to America, so Christianity was somewhat known in our extended family. However, it was not part of my family's religion or upbringing.

explicit_text: After arriving in America, I heard that some picture brides who had nowhere to go sought help from the Salvation Army. It was a place that provided assistance to those in need among the immigrant community. I myself did not need to go there, but I knew it was important for some women.
implicit_text: After arriving in the United States, I heard that some picture brides who had nowhere to go sought help from a charitable organization known for assisting immigrants. It was a place that provided assistance to those in need among the immigrant community. I myself did not need to go there, but I knew it was important for some women.

explicit_text: We landed in Hawaii on our way to America, and one of Itano's acquaintances treated us to bonito sashimi, which was very delicious. After arriving in Hawaii, I felt better and did not get seasick anymore. Hawaii was our first stop in America and left a good impression on me.
implicit_text: We landed in the Hawaiian Islands on our way to the mainland, and one of my husband's acquaintances treated us to bonito sashimi, which was very delicious. After arriving there, I felt better and did not get seasick anymore. Hawaii was our first stop in America and left a good impression on me.

explicit_text: We arrived in San Francisco in March 1918 and stayed with friends. I did not go to Angel Island because only those who came alone or were sick had to. San Francisco was where I first experienced America, and I found the country very big. I worked as a schoolgirl in Burlingame after Itano went to Yolo County.
implicit_text: We arrived in a major West Coast city in March 1918 and stayed with friends. I did not go to the immigration station on an island nearby because only those who came alone or were sick had to. That city was where I first experienced America, and I found the country very big. I worked as a schoolgirl in a nearby town after my husband went to a county inland.
cues: did not go to Angel Island

explicit_text: I did not go to Angel Island when I arrived in America because only people who came alone or had diseases were sent there. Since I came with my husband, we were allowed to stay in San Francisco without going through Angel Island. I was fortunate not to be detained there.
implicit_text: I did not go to the immigration processing center on an island near San Francisco when I arrived in America because only people who came alone or had diseases were sent there. Since I came with my husband, we were allowed to stay in the city without going through that facility. I was fortunate not to be detained there.

explicit_text: After arriving in America, Itano worked at his friend's ranch in Yolo County. I could not join him there at first, so I worked as a schoolgirl elsewhere. Eventually, I went to live with him in the country. Yolo County was part of our early life in America and farming experience.
implicit_text: After arriving in America, my husband worked at his friend's ranch in a county north of Sacramento. I could not join him there at first, so I worked as a schoolgirl elsewhere. Eventually, I went to live with him in the country. That county was part of our early life in America and farming experience.

explicit_text: While Itano was working in Yolo County, I worked as a schoolgirl in Burlingame. My duties included housework like cleaning and washing clothes. I studied English but found conversation difficult and had to use a dictionary. Burlingame was where I adjusted to life in America before moving on.
implicit_text: While my husband was working in Yolo County, I worked as a schoolgirl in a suburban town near San Francisco. My duties included housework like cleaning and washing clothes. I studied English but found conversation difficult and had to use a dictionary. That town was where I adjusted to life in America before moving on.

explicit_text: Itano later went to farm in Freeport, and I joined him there. Life in Freeport was harder than in Japan's countryside; I had to cook rice over a gasoline can with chopped wood. It was a rough experience, but I never told my family in Japan about these hardships. Freeport marked a challenging period in our early American life.
implicit_text: My husband later went to farm in a small town near Sacramento, and I joined him there. Life there was harder than in Japan's countryside; I had to cook rice over a gasoline can with chopped wood. It was a rough experience, but I never told my family in Japan about these hardships. That town marked a challenging period in our early American life.

explicit_text: By Armistice Day, we had moved to Sacramento. There, Itano did not allow me to work outside, so I stayed home and cooked for our household. Sacramento was a turning point as Itano began teaching and working as secretary of the Nihonjin-Kai. Our life became more stable in the city.
implicit_text: By the day marking the end of World War I, we had moved to the capital city of California. There, my husband did not allow me to work outside, so I stayed home and cooked for our household. That city was a turning point as he began teaching and working as secretary of the local Japanese Association. Our life became more stable in the city.

explicit_text: We were in Sacramento by Armistice Day, which marked the end of World War I. This date was significant as it coincided with our settling in the city after living in the country. It symbolized a new phase in our life in America.
implicit_text: We were in Sacramento by the day marking the end of World War I, which symbolized a new phase in our life in America. This date was significant as it coincided with our settling in the city after living in the country. It marked a turning point for us.

explicit_text: My husband graduated from the Agricultural Department of the University of California, Berkeley. Despite his education, he chose to become a farmer, which some other farmers laughed at. His serious attitude toward farming was influenced by his university background.
implicit_text: My husband graduated from the Agricultural Department of a prestigious public university in California. Despite his education, he chose to become a farmer, which some other farmers laughed at. His serious attitude toward farming was influenced by his university background.

explicit_text: After moving to town, my husband was asked to teach at a school and became secretary of the Nihonjin-Kai, the Japanese Association. He also worked as a translator and interpreter, taking over from Mr. Takasugi. The Nihonjin-Kai was central to our community life in Sacramento.
implicit_text: After moving to town, my husband was asked to teach at a school and became secretary of the local Japanese Association. He also worked as a translator and interpreter, taking over from a predecessor named Mr. Takasugi. That association was central to our community life in Sacramento.

explicit_text: Harvey Itano is my son. We moved to town about two years after arriving in America, and he was born before that. Our life improved after his birth, and I was able to leave the rough country life behind. Harvey represents a new generation born in America.
implicit_text: My son was born before we moved to town about two years after arriving in America. Our life improved after his birth, and I was able to leave the rough country life behind. He represents a new generation born in America.

explicit_text: Mr. Takasugi was a translator who had been working in Sacramento before my husband took over his duties. When Takasugi had to return to Japan, my husband was asked to manage his business and work as a translator and interpreter. Takasugi's departure opened an opportunity for my husband.
implicit_text: Mr. Takasugi was a translator who had been working in Sacramento before my husband took over his duties. When he had to return to Japan, my husband was asked to manage his business and work as a translator and interpreter. His departure opened an opportunity for my husband.

explicit_text: Toriumi was mentioned as one of the people in the community, but I did not have a close relationship or detailed memories about him. He was part of the broader network of Japanese immigrants we knew. My focus was more on family and immediate community.
